mapping.a_element: modular-Laplacian cellular-automaton growth on a 2D lattice

mapping.b_element: self-similar fractal cluster characterized by a fractal dimension

brief_justification: The overlap is only the shared problem-domain of 2D fractal growth and the word 'Laplacian': paper A is a deterministic modular-arithmetic cellular automaton whose morphology is set by mask geometry and steered by discretely timed ternary interventions found via Monte Carlo optimization, whereas paper B is a stochastic Hastings-Levitov conformal-map aggregation analyzed through the multifractal spectrum of the harmonic measure and an exact third-moment-to-fractal-dimension amplitude relation. The governing processes and analytical machinery differ, and no role-for-role correspondence (e.g. no analog of harmonic measure/conformal maps in A, none of modular interventions in B) coherently holds.
